title: trifolium purpureum var. laxiusculum
intro: Trifolium purpureum var. laxiusculum, also known as the perennial red clover, is a variety of the red clover plant, a legume native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiviral, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects. The perennial red clover has been associated with various health benefits, such as reducing menopausal symptoms, alleviating allergy symptoms, and supporting bone health due to its isoflavone content. In herbal preparations, the perennial red clover is often used in teas, infusions, and capsules, as well as in topical applications like creams and ointments for skin issues.
